taxonomy.problem: Users cannot connect to Lattice SQL pools; logins/handshakes time out or reset, and errors indicate SAS token retrieval failures (moniker not active) and AccessRejectedForUserConnections.
taxonomy.node_label: SQL pool connections fail due to token errors

taxonomy.raw_action_string: Transferred from CloudAnalytics/Connectivity-Security to CloudAnalytics/Client Experiences.
taxonomy.description:
What: An incident routing/ownership change: the ticket was transferred from the CloudAnalytics/Connectivity-Security team to the CloudAnalytics/Client Experiences team.
Why/Purpose: The summary indicates SAS token retrieval failures with a MonikerNotFoundException; transferring to a team better aligned with client experience/connectivity symptoms and to involve DXS for root cause analysis.
Expected outcome: Get the appropriate owning team engaged to investigate the SAS token retrieval/moniker activation issue and drive resolution.
Actual findings/conclusions: No technical conclusion is stated here beyond the rationale that the failures relate to SAS token retrieval and moniker not active.
Impact on troubleshooting: Ensured the incident was handled by the team(s) most likely to diagnose and fix the underlying platform/service issue.
taxonomy.node_label: Transfer incident to client experiences team

taxonomy.problem: After transferring to DXS, investigation continues into SAS token retrieval failures (MonikerNotFoundException) and resulting login rejections/connection resets impacting SQL pool connectivity.
taxonomy.node_label: Investigate moniker token failures causing login resets

taxonomy.raw_action_string: AppTrace | where MessageText has_all ( '[ANONYMIZED_GUID]' ) | where TIMESTAMP between ( datetime (2023-09-12 00:20:27.6164291).. datetime (2023-09-12 23:20:27.6164291)) | where ProcessName == 'Contoso.Dxs.Service.EntryPoint' | where MarkerName has 'TdsSession' | project TIMESTAMP , RootActivityId , MarkerName
taxonomy.description:
What: Runs a KQL query against the AppTrace table to find TdsSession-related trace events for a specific workspace identifier within a precise time window, scoped to the DXS service entry point process, and projects timestamp plus RootActivityId (RAID) and marker.
Why/Purpose: The engineer was troubleshooting connection/login failures and needed to correlate the failing client/workspace to server-side traces; specifically, to obtain the RootActivityId associated with the TdsSession for later drill-down.
Expected outcome: Return rows containing the RootActivityId(s) for the workspace during the incident timeframe.
Actual findings/conclusions: The ticket notes the query returned a schema including [TIMESTAMP, RootActivityId, MarkerName], implying results were obtained and RootActivityId was available for follow-up.
Impact on troubleshooting: Produced the RootActivityId needed to pivot into a deeper trace query (next action) and later extract activity/connection context.
taxonomy.possible_outcome: AppTrace query returned columns [TIMESTAMP, RootActivityId, MarkerName], enabling identification of relevant RootActivityIds for the failing sessions.

taxonomy.raw_action_string: AppTrace | where RootActivityId in ( '[ANONYMIZED_GUID]' ) | project TIMESTAMP , Severity , RootActivityId , ProcessName , MarkerName , ThreadId , MessageText
taxonomy.description:
What: Queries AppTrace again, this time filtering by RootActivityId (RAID) to retrieve detailed trace messages (severity, process, marker, thread, message text) for that activity.
Why/Purpose: After identifying the relevant RootActivityId from the prior AppTrace search, the engineer drilled into the full trace context to understand what happened during the failing session.
Expected outcome: Detailed trace lines for the specific RootActivityId to reveal errors or authentication/connection handling details.
Actual findings/conclusions: The ticket notes the query returned a schema including [TIMESTAMP, Severity, RootActivityId, ProcessName, MarkerName, ThreadId, MessageText], indicating trace details were retrieved and used to proceed (later they extracted an Activity id from the prior Kusto result).
Impact on troubleshooting: Enabled extraction of lower-level connection diagnostics (e.g., activity/connection identifiers) used to pivot into SQL/Auth logs.
taxonomy.trigger_observation: AppTrace query returned columns [TIMESTAMP, RootActivityId, MarkerName], enabling identification of relevant RootActivityIds for the failing sessions.
taxonomy.possible_outcome: AppTrace drill-down query returned columns including [Severity, ProcessName, MessageText], allowing deeper inspection of the failing login/session trace details.

taxonomy.raw_action_string:
AuthLogin
| where TIMESTAMP > ago (10d)
| where peer_activity_id == "[ANONYMIZED_GUID]"
| project TIMESTAMP , logical_server_name , connection_id , connection_peer_id , instance_name , server_name , ClusterName , AppName , state_desc
taxonomy.description:
What: Queries the AuthLogin table for the last 10 days and filters to a specific peer_activity_id (activity id), projecting connection/server metadata and state.
Why/Purpose: The engineer observed AccessRejectedForUserConnections in SQL logs and used the Activity id (noted as coming from the previous Kusto result) to locate the corresponding authentication/login record(s) and determine where/why the connection was rejected.
Expected outcome: AuthLogin entries tied to the specific activity id, showing server/cluster/app and the rejection state.
Actual findings/conclusions: The ticket states they see AccessRejectedForUserConnections when checking SQL logs; the query returned a schema including state_desc, implying they retrieved relevant login records for analysis.
Impact on troubleshooting: Correlated the client-side failure to server-side authentication rejection events, supporting escalation/transfer to the connectivity/security team.
taxonomy.trigger_observation: AppTrace drill-down query returned columns including [Severity, ProcessName, MessageText], allowing deeper inspection of the failing login/session trace details.
taxonomy.possible_outcome: AuthLogin query returned columns including [logical_server_name, connection_id, ClusterName, AppName, state_desc], supporting verification of AccessRejectedForUserConnections during login.
taxonomy.node_label: Query AuthLogin by activity ID for rejection

taxonomy.raw_action_string: Transferred from CloudAnalytics/Client Experiences to CloudAnalytics/Connectivity-Security.
taxonomy.description:
What: Incident ownership/routing transfer.
Why/Purpose: After observing AccessRejectedForUserConnections during connection attempts (suggesting an auth/connectivity/security gate rather than an application-only issue), the engineer transferred the incident to the team best suited to investigate connection rejection causes.
Expected outcome: The receiving team (Connectivity-Security) would validate why connections are being rejected and take remediation steps.
Actual findings/conclusions: Transfer itself indicates the investigation concluded the issue likely falls under connectivity/security controls.
Impact on troubleshooting: Ensured the incident was handled by the appropriate specialized team to resolve AccessRejectedForUserConnections.
taxonomy.trigger_observation: AuthLogin query returned columns including [logical_server_name, connection_id, ClusterName, AppName, state_desc], supporting verification of AccessRejectedForUserConnections during login.
taxonomy.possible_outcome: Request raised to verify why connections are rejected with AccessRejectedForUserConnections; no direct outcome captured in the transcript.
taxonomy.node_label: Transfer incident to connectivity security team

taxonomy.description:
What: Runs a simple SQL statement to return the SQL Server version string from the connected workspace/instance.
Why: Suggested as a lightweight connectivity/health check before running stress workloads, to ensure the workspace is active and accepting connections.
Purpose: Validate that SSMS can successfully connect and execute a trivial query, indicating the workspace is operational.
Expected outcome: A successful result containing the server/version information; if it fails, retry until it succeeds.
Actual findings/conclusions: No specific result is recorded in the ticket text; it is presented as recommended procedure.
Impact on troubleshooting: Helps distinguish workspace activation/connectivity issues from workload-related failures and reduces risk of running stress while the workspace is not fully active.
taxonomy.trigger_observation: Request raised to verify why connections are rejected with AccessRejectedForUserConnections; no direct outcome captured in the transcript.
taxonomy.possible_outcome: Proposed validation step: use SSMS to run a simple query to confirm workspace is active before stress runs; no execution result recorded.

taxonomy.raw_action_string: AuthLogin | where ClientConnectionId == '11111111-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-111111111111' | project TIMESTAMP, error, state
taxonomy.description:
What: Queries the AuthLogin telemetry/table, filtering to a specific ClientConnectionId, and projects timestamp plus error/state fields.
Why: Executed to confirm why a particular session/connection was rejected (i.e., to retrieve the rejection reason associated with that connection attempt).
Purpose: Diagnose the failing login/session by looking up authentication/login records tied to the provided ClientConnectionId.
Expected outcome: Rows showing the relevant login attempt(s) with associated error and state, enabling identification of the rejection reason.
Actual findings/conclusions: The text states it was run "to confirm the connection rejection reason" but does not include the returned error/state values.
Impact on troubleshooting: Narrows the investigation to an authentication/authorization/resource-access rejection cause for the specific failing connection, supporting root-cause tracking of the "zombie SQL" login failure scenario.

KQL literal-value dependency analysis:
- Literal values found in predicates: ClientConnectionId == '11111111-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-111111111111'
- Backward scan: No earlier extracted action in this ticket reports discovering this ClientConnectionId; it is introduced directly here.
- Dependency: none.
taxonomy.trigger_observation: Proposed validation step: use SSMS to run a simple query to confirm workspace is active before stress runs; no execution result recorded.
taxonomy.possible_outcome: AuthLogin lookup was run for a failing ClientConnectionId to confirm the rejection reason; specific lookup output not captured.
taxonomy.node_label: Query AuthLogin by ClientConnectionId for reason

taxonomy.cause: Disabling SQL frontend auto-deactivation can leave the FE up while DXS assumes inactivity and evicts the moniker; then DXS stops handing back tokens, leading to SAS token retrieval failures and subsequent AccessRejectedForUserConnections ('Only backend connections are allowed').
taxonomy.node_label: Re-enable frontend auto-deactivation to prevent moniker eviction
